zones.name: Earth
zones.description: The planet where we live, complete with land, water, and air. The third planet from the sun.

zones.name: Atmosphere
zones.description: The layer of air that surrounds our planet, composed primarily of nitrogen and oxygen.

zones.name: Low Earth Orbit (LEO)
zones.description: The area in space closest to Earth, typically 200-2,000 km above the surface.

zones.name: Medium Earth Orbit (MEO)
zones.description: Higher than LEO but closer than HEO, approximately 2,000-35,786 km above Earth.

zones.name: High Earth Orbit (HEO)
zones.description: Farthest from Earth among the three main orbit types, above 35,786 km.

zones.name: Space Between HEO and Moon Orbit
zones.description: The cis-lunar space extending from geostationary orbit to the Moon's orbit.

zones.name: Moon Orbit
zones.description: The orbital region around the Moon, approximately 384,400 km from Earth.

zones.name: The Moon
zones.description: Earth's only natural satellite, where the first human landing occurred in 1969.

zones.name: The Mearth Line
zones.description: A conceptual sphere with a 297,000-mile radius encompassing the Moon-Earth system.

zones.name: Space Between Moon and Mars
zones.description: Interplanetary space lying between the Moon's orbit and Mars's atmosphere.

zones.name: Mars Atmosphere/Orbit
zones.description: The thin Martian atmosphere and orbital region around the Red Planet.

zones.name: Mars
zones.description: The fourth planet from the Sun, often called the Red Planet.

zones.name: Phobos
zones.description: The larger of Mars's two moons, known for its irregular shape.

zones.name: Deimos
zones.description: The smaller and more distant of Mars's two moons.

zones.name: Beyond
zones.description: Everything else in the Universe beyond the immediate scope of the Mearth system.

metadata.note: The Project Moon Hut Classification System defines 15 geographic zones from Earth to Beyond, representing different operational regions in the expanding Mearth ecosystem.
